title: alnus rugosa
intro: Alnus rugosa, also known as the American speckled alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The medicinal properties of Alnus rugosa lie in its bark, which contains tannins and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Alnus rugosa include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Alnus rugosa can be used in herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
